Afterimage Strike.effect: Auto-dodges every incoming melee attack for the duration (does not evade Ki blasts).
Afterimage Strike.summary: 3 skill stock, ~10s full-auto melee dodge. The signature launch-era degeneracy; dismantled across four patches but still usable.

changeHistory.change: Duration 15s; dodges all melee. Dominant ranked tool (community-measured).
Duration 15s → 10s; now ends if the opponent lands a Perception. [official]
Now also ends if the user is hit by a Super Counter. [official]
General unblockable Rush Blasts become evadable by auto-evasion skills (incl. Afterimage). [official]
Skill-description text fixed to match effect; community-reported deletion on Perception counter reaffirmed. [community]
Confirmed ends on Super Counter; can now evade unblockable charge Blasts (small buff). [official]

Afterimage Strike.body: Afterimage Strike is the most patched skill in the game. At launch it cost **3 skill stock** and granted a **15-second** window that auto-dodged *every* melee attack (but never Ki blasts), making it the defining cheese of the "Wild West" meta. The Dec 11 2024 update cut the duration to **10 seconds** and made it **end when the opponent lands a Perception**; the Jun 23 2025 patch added a second hard counter (**ending it on a Super Counter**); and by the May 26 2026 patch it is firmly a situational tool rather than an oppressive one — though that same patch let it evade unblockable charge Blasts. A circulating "single-dodge per activation" claim is false: both the datamine and official text describe continuous melee evasion for the full 10s.

Wild Sense.effect: Persistent one-hit auto-dodge that counters the next melee attack, then is consumed on trigger.
Wild Sense.summary: 2 skill stock, single auto-dodge + counter (consumed on trigger). Heavily restricted Dec 2025 — 'Wild Sense is GONE'.

changeHistory.change: Top-tier auto-dodge tool alongside Afterimage Strike.
Attack damage reduced; can no longer be used while continuing a combo with a Rush-In. [official]
Cancelled if the opponent uses Perception during its activation. [official]
Heavily restricted ('Wild Sense is GONE' per community); can no longer auto-dodge mid-combo continuation. [community]

Wild Sense.body: Wild Sense is a **2 skill stock** reactive defense that auto-dodges a single incoming melee attack and counters, consuming the buff on trigger. Unlike Afterimage Strike (a timed multi-dodge), it is a one-shot read. It was a launch-era staple; the Jan 20 2025 patch cut its counter damage and barred its use while continuing a combo via Rush-In, the Apr 21 2025 patch let Perception cancel it, and the Dec 15 2025 patch restricted it so severely that the community declared it effectively dead for combo-continuation defense.

Senzu Beans.summary: Full-heal skill. Cost raised 5→6 and starting stock 2→0 in the first patch, killing the senzu-stall meta.

changeHistory.change: Cost 5 skill stock for a FULL heal; Yajirobe started with 2 stock — enabled an infinite-heal stall (carried over from Budokai Tenkaichi 3, which also used 5).
Senzu cost 5 → 6; Yajirobe starting skill stock 2 → 0; max skill count raised 5 → 6; HP reduced ~½ a bar. Took Yajirobe 'best to worst overnight'. [official/community]
Yajirobe Ki-recovery speed reduced (alongside Roshi/Mr. Satan). [official]

Senzu Beans.body: Senzu Beans is Yajirobe's signature skill: a **full health restore**. At launch it cost **5 skill stock** and Yajirobe began the match with 2 stock, enabling a notorious infinite-heal stall. The very first balance patch (late Oct 2024) raised the cost to **6 stock**, set his starting stock to **0**, raised his max skill count 5→6, and shaved about half a health bar — a change widely described as taking him 'from best to worst overnight'. The May 26 2026 patch further reduced his Ki-recovery, and because Single Battle now uses standardized HP, his tank value there is gone; he survives only as a niche DP-Battle body.

Explosive Wave.effect: AoE energy burst that knocks back nearby opponents and clears incoming ki blasts.
Explosive Wave.summary: AoE knockback / ki-clear burst. Exists in 2-cost and 3-cost variants depending on character.
Explosive Wave.body: Explosive Wave is a close-range area-of-effect burst that knocks back surrounding opponents and clears incoming ki blasts, used as a get-off-me / space-reset tool. Research notes both **2-cost and 3-cost** variants exist depending on the character. No specific balance changes to it are documented in the official patch notes through June 2026.

Instant Transmission.effect: Teleports the user to reposition instantly (skill-stock cost is character-specific).
Instant Transmission.summary: Skill-stock teleport reposition; one of the documented counters to distance-scaled Z-Burst Dash pressure.

changeHistory.change: Listed among the counters to the reworked (distance-scaled) Z-Burst Dash pressure. [official/community]

Instant Transmission.body: Instant Transmission is a teleport reposition skill (cost character-specific) used to escape pressure or close/create distance. Its exact skill-stock cost is not cleanly datamined. After the May 26 2026 Z-Burst Dash rework (initial Ki cost lowered but now scaling with travel distance), Instant Transmission is cited as one of the explicit counters to full-screen Z-Burst approaches alongside Dragon-Dash descend and switch-just-before-hit.

Solar Flare.effect: Blinds the opponent, interrupting their actions for a short window.
Solar Flare.summary: Blind/stun utility. Jan 2026 patch made it persist through Dragon Dash / Z-Burst Dash approaches.

changeHistory.change: No longer ends when the opponent approaches via Dragon Dash / Z-Burst Dash — the blind now persists through dashes. [official]

Solar Flare.body: Solar Flare blinds the opponent, briefly interrupting their inputs to open a punish or escape window. The January 26 2026 patch buffed it so that it no longer ends when the opponent rushes in with a Dragon Dash or Z-Burst Dash — previously, dashing through cancelled the blind; now it persists, making it a more reliable neutral disruption tool.

Kaioken.effect: Multiplies the user's power for a window (Goku Z-Early), at the cost of self-strain.
Kaioken.summary: Goku (Z-Early) power-multiplier buff; skill-stock cost was reduced in the Jan 20 2025 patch.

changeHistory.change: Skill Count cost reduced (Goku Z-Early only). [official]

Kaioken.body: Kaioken is Goku (Z - Early)'s power-amplification skill, multiplying his attack output for a window. The January 20 2025 patch reduced its Skill Count cost, making it cheaper to activate. Its exact current stock cost is not published numerically in the official notes.

False Courage.summary: Self-buff skill; Skill Count cost raised 1→2 in the Dec 2024 patch.

changeHistory.change: Skill Count cost increased 1 → 2. [official exact] (listed as 'False Courage' / 'Force Courage' across sources)

False Courage.body: False Courage is a self-buff activation skill. The December 11 2024 update raised its Skill Count cost from **1 to 2**, making it more expensive to pop. (Community patch breakdowns also refer to a 'Force Courage 1→2' change in the Dec 2025 cleanup; the official Dec 2024 note is the firmly-sourced 1→2 adjustment.)

All I Need Is Five Seconds!.effect: Activation stat boost with full Ki recovery for a short burst.
All I Need Is Five Seconds!.summary: Burst self-buff. Cost cut 3→2 + boost up (Dec 2024); now also fully recovers Ki (Jan 2025).

changeHistory.change: Skill Count cost 3 → 2 and activation stat boost increased. [official exact]
Ki now fully recovered on activation. [official]

All I Need Is Five Seconds!.body: 'All I Need Is Five Seconds!' is a burst self-buff. The December 11 2024 update cut its Skill Count cost from **3 to 2** and increased the activation stat boost; the January 20 2025 patch then added **full Ki recovery** on activation, turning it into a strong tempo/comeback button for the Trunks line.

Saiyan Spirit.effect: Timed attack/stat buff for Saiyan characters.
Saiyan Spirit.summary: Timed Saiyan buff; duration increased in the Jun 2025 and May 2026 patches.

changeHistory.change: Buff duration increased (alongside Pump Up). [official]
Buff duration increased again (alongside Pump Up). [official]

Saiyan Spirit.body: Saiyan Spirit is a timed self-buff. Both the June 23 2025 and May 26 2026 patches increased buff-skill durations (Saiyan Spirit and Pump Up are explicitly named), lengthening the effective window of the boost.

Pump Up.summary: Timed self-buff; duration increased in the Jun 2025 and May 2026 patches.

changeHistory.change: Buff duration increased (alongside Saiyan Spirit). [official]
Buff duration increased again (alongside Saiyan Spirit). [official]

Pump Up.body: Pump Up is a timed self-buff that raises the user's output for a window. It was lengthened in both the June 23 2025 and May 26 2026 patches, which increased buff-skill durations generally (Pump Up and Saiyan Spirit named explicitly).

Fruit of the Tree of Might.effect: Large stat boost plus Ki recovery (Turles).
Fruit of the Tree of Might.summary: Turles buff: enlarged boost + Ki recovery in the Jun 2025 / May 2026 patches.

changeHistory.change: Bigger boost + Ki recovery added. [official]
Bigger boost + Ki recovery reaffirmed. [official]

Fruit of the Tree of Might.body: Turles's Fruit of the Tree of Might is a self-buff that, since the June 23 2025 patch, grants a bigger boost and Ki recovery on use — reaffirmed in the May 26 2026 update.

Cry of Rage.summary: Kale's berserk trigger; activation time increased (a nerf) in the May 26 2026 patch.

changeHistory.change: Activation time increased (nerf to Kale's berserk access). [official]

Cry of Rage.body: Cry of Rage is Kale's berserker activation. The May 26 2026 patch increased its activation time, slowing how quickly she can enter her dominant Berserk state — a targeted nerf, since Kale (Berserk) is a top-tier Singles pick (#9, ~129.1k on sparkingzerometa) and strong in DP value too.

Super Maximum Light Speed Mode.summary: Dyspo speed mode; May 2026 patch reduced its Short-Dash Ki cost and added an icon.

changeHistory.change: Ki cost for Short Dash reduced while active; status icon added. [official]

Super Maximum Light Speed Mode.body: Dyspo's Super Maximum Light Speed Mode is a speed/mobility buff. The May 26 2026 patch reduced the Ki cost of Short Dash while it is active and added a status icon so its uptime is readable.

Time Skip.effect: Hit's time-stop technique that briefly freezes the opponent.
Time Skip.summary: Hit's signature time-stop opener; no documented balance changes through June 2026.
Time Skip.body: Time Skip is Hit's signature technique, briefly freezing the opponent to set up a punish. No specific numeric balance changes to it appear in the official patch notes through June 2026; Hit himself is a high-ATK 'Rival class' body (DP8).

Regeneration.effect: Restores a portion of the user's health (Namekian/Buu/Cell regenerators).
Regeneration.summary: Self-heal for regenerating characters; pairs with revival/regen kits noted on Cooler/Metal Cooler.
Regeneration.body: Regeneration restores a portion of health for characters with regenerating bodies (Namekians, Buu line, Cell). It underpins the durable 'revival/regeneration' kits that research highlights for Cooler and Metal Cooler. No discrete numeric patch changes are documented for the skill itself through June 2026.

Meditation.effect: Channels to recover Ki and/or skill resources while stationary.
Meditation.summary: Stationary Ki/skill recovery channel; no documented balance changes through June 2026.
Meditation.body: Meditation is a stationary channel used to recover resources. No specific numeric balance changes to it are documented in the official notes through June 2026; it sits within the broader skill-stock economy that the May 26 2026 patch tuned (auto-recovery slightly reduced).

Ta-dah!.summary: Gotenks skill; Skill Count cost reduced in the Jan 20 2025 patch.

changeHistory.change: Skill Count cost reduced. [official]

Ta-dah!.body: 'Ta-dah!' is one of Gotenks's signature skills. The January 20 2025 patch reduced its Skill Count cost, making it cheaper to use.

Full Power.summary: Generic max-power activation buff; transform-buff economy reshaped by the Sep 2025 / May 2026 permanent-ATK-on-transform model.

changeHistory.change: Transform-on-Skill-Stock now grants a permanent ATK buff (removed on revert); transform-heal removed in Single Battle. [official]
Permanent-ATK-buff-on-transform model locked in cross-platform. [official]

Full Power.body: Full Power is a self power-up activation. Its strategic value shifted with the September 22 2025 transformation overhaul: in Single Battle, transforming via Skill Stock no longer heals but instead grants a **permanent attack-power buff** (lost on revert) — a model re-locked across all platforms in the May 26 2026 update. This reframes power-up skills as a lasting offense investment rather than a panic-heal.

Time to Get Serious.summary: Self-buff activation; governed by the same transform/buff economy reshaped in 2025–2026.
Time to Get Serious.body: 'Time to Get Serious' is a self-buff activation skill. No discrete numeric balance change is documented for it specifically; it operates inside the broader buff/transform economy that the 2025–2026 patches reshaped (permanent-ATK-on-transform, longer buff durations).

Fury.summary: Anger self-buff; no discrete numeric patch change documented through June 2026.
Fury.body: Fury is an anger-driven self power-up. No specific numeric balance change is documented for it in the official notes through June 2026.

Immortal Body.effect: Damage-resistance / survivability buff associated with regenerating/immortal characters.
Immortal Body.summary: Survivability buff tied to the Zamasu line; no discrete numeric patch change documented.
Immortal Body.body: Immortal Body is a survivability buff associated with immortal/regenerating characters such as the Zamasu line. No discrete numeric balance change is documented for it through June 2026.
